class.name: VirtualDevice
class.brief_description: Abstract base class for multitouch virtual input devices.
class.description:
[VirtualDevice] is an abstract base class that provides multitouch support for virtual input controls like joysticks, buttons, and D-pads. It inherits from [Control], enabling layout and theming.
[b]Input Modes:[/b] The device can operate in two distinct modes controlled by [member input_mode]: - [constant INPUT_MODE_NATIVE]: Dispatches real [InputEventVirtualButton] and [InputEventVirtualMotion] events through [Input]. This is the default, high-performance path that integrates with the [InputMap] and works transparently with [method Input.is_action_pressed]. - [constant INPUT_MODE_EMULATED]: Simpler emulation path, similar to [TouchScreenButton]. Instead of native virtual device events, it fires standard [InputEventAction] events. Useful for quick prototyping or when native virtual device support is not available.
[b]Output control in Emulated mode:[/b] Use [member emit_input_events] and [member emit_signals] to independently toggle whether the emulated mode feeds the [Input] system and/or emits GDScript signals ([signal action_pressed], [signal action_released]).
[b]Multitouch Architecture:[/b] Each device tracks a single touch index to prevent conflicts between different virtual devices. The system uses: -1 for no touch, -2 for mouse input, and 0+ for individual finger touches. This ensures that multiple virtual devices on screen don't interfere with each other when users touch different areas simultaneously.
Use this class as a base if you want to implement custom virtual input devices with proper multitouch isolation. Subclasses must implement the virtual touch handlers (_on_touch_down, _on_touch_up, _on_drag) to define their specific behavior.

method.name: get_active_touch_count
method.description: Returns the number of currently active touches on this device.

method.name: get_current_touch_index
method.description: Returns the current touch index tracking this device. Returns -1 if no touch is active, -2 for mouse input, or 0+ for finger touches. Useful for debugging multitouch behavior.

method.name: get_draw_mode
method.description: Returns the current visual state of the device (normal, pressed, hovered, etc.), useful for custom drawing logic.

method.name: get_touch_duration
method.description: Returns the duration in seconds that the specified touch has been active. Returns 0.0 if the touch is not active.

method.name: get_touch_position
method.description: Returns the current position of the specified touch. Returns Vector2.ZERO if the touch is not active.

method.name: get_touch_pressure
method.description: Returns the pressure value of the specified touch (0.0 to 1.0). Returns 0.0 if the touch is not active.

method.name: get_touch_start_position
method.description: Returns the initial position where the specified touch started. Returns Vector2.ZERO if the touch is not active.

method.name: is_hovered
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the mouse is hovering over the device.

method.name: is_pressed
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the device is currently being touched or clicked.

method.name: is_touch_active
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the specified touch index is currently active on this device.

members.member: The device index to be used in the generated [InputEvent]. For example, if set to 0, events will appear as "Device 0".

If [code]true[/code], the device feeds [InputEventAction] events into the [Input] system when operating in [constant INPUT_MODE_EMULATED]. Disable this to suppress input propagation while keeping GDScript signals active. [b]Note:[/b] Has no effect in [constant INPUT_MODE_NATIVE] mode.

If [code]true[/code], the device emits [signal action_pressed] and [signal action_released] signals when operating in [constant INPUT_MODE_EMULATED]. Disable this to suppress signal emission while keeping input event propagation active. [b]Note:[/b] Has no effect in [constant INPUT_MODE_NATIVE] mode.

Controls how the device generates input events. - [constant INPUT_MODE_NATIVE]: Dispatches [InputEventVirtualButton] / [InputEventVirtualMotion] through [Input]. Fully integrated with [InputMap]. - [constant INPUT_MODE_EMULATED]: Dispatches standard [InputEventAction] events, similar to [TouchScreenButton]. Controlled by [member emit_input_events] and [member emit_signals].
The maximum number of simultaneous touches this device can track (1 to 32).
If [code]true[/code], the first touch to touch the device becomes the primary touch. If [code]false[/code], the last touch becomes primary.
Controls when the virtual device is visible. Can be set to show always or only on touchscreen devices.

signal.name: action_pressed
signal.description: Emitted when an emulated action is activated. Only fires in [constant INPUT_MODE_EMULATED] when [member emit_signals] is [code]true[/code].

signal.name: action_released
signal.description: Emitted when an emulated action is deactivated. Only fires in [constant INPUT_MODE_EMULATED] when [member emit_signals] is [code]true[/code].

constants.constant: The device is only visible if the [DisplayServer] supports touch input.
Dispatches real [InputEventVirtualButton] and [InputEventVirtualMotion] events through [Input]. Fully integrated with [InputMap]; use [method Input.is_action_pressed] as usual.
Simpler emulation path that dispatches standard [InputEventAction] events, similar to [TouchScreenButton]. Output is controlled by [member emit_input_events] and [member emit_signals].
